# UC-14.3.1 ┬╖ Temperature Anomaly Detection

## Description

Edge-based kNN anomaly detection provides faster response than cloud-based processing for critical temperature monitoring in data centers and industrial environments.

## Value

Edge-based kNN anomaly detection provides faster response than cloud-based processing for critical temperature monitoring in data centers and industrial environments.

## Implementation

Deploy Edge Hub device (IP66-rated, built-in temperature sensor ┬▒0.2┬░C accuracy). Enable kNN anomaly detection via the Edge Hub mobile app тАФ toggle "Anomaly Detection" on the temperature sensor tile. Sensor data streams as metrics to `edge-hub-data` index; anomalies to `edge-hub-anomalies` index via HEC. Create alerts on anomaly count spikes. Optional: attach external I┬▓C temperature probes via the 3.5mm jack for additional measurement points.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
тАв Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (built-in kNN model), Splunk OT Intelligence.
тАв Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `index=edge-hub-anomalies` (anomaly metrics), `sourcetype=edge_hub`.
тАв For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
Deploy Edge Hub device (IP66-rated, built-in temperature sensor ┬▒0.2┬░C accuracy). Enable kNN anomaly detection via the Edge Hub mobile app тАФ toggle "Anomaly Detection" on the temperature sensor tile. Sensor data streams as metrics to `edge-hub-data` index; anomalies to `edge-hub-anomalies` index via HEC. Create alerts on anomaly count spikes. Optional: attach external I┬▓C temperature probes via the 3.5mm jack for additional measurement points.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats count where index=edge-hub-anomalies AND metric_name=temperature AND type="anomaly-detector"
  span=1h by extracted_host
| where count > 0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Temperature Anomaly Detection** тАФ Edge-based kNN anomaly detection provides faster response than cloud-based processing for critical temperature monitoring in data centers and industrial environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `index=edge-hub-anomalies` (anomaly metrics), `sourcetype=edge_hub`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (built-in kNN model), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedтАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-anomalies.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

тАв Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
тАв Filters the current rows with `where count > 0` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 тАФ Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (mstats temperature trend by device), Single value (current temperature), Timeline (anomaly events from edge-hub-anomalies).

## SPL

```spl
| mstats count where index=edge-hub-anomalies AND metric_name=temperature AND type="anomaly-detector"
  span=1h by extracted_host
| where count > 0
```

## Visualization

Line chart (mstats temperature trend by device), Single value (current temperature), Timeline (anomaly events from edge-hub-anomalies).
